title: README Rewrite — Marketing-Quality with Visual Assets and Research References

success_looks_like: README opens with hero GIF placeholder + factual tagline (no 'revolutionary')
Pipeline diagram using Mermaid with a worked example showing a directive flowing through the 15 steps
Research citations inline-linked to Anthropic context engineering, OpenAI harness engineering, Anthropic multi-agent research
Competitor comparison table against agent frameworks (CrewAI, AutoGen, Devin, Manus, etc.) — no coding assistants
CEO-as-CEO narrative: give directives, agents brainstorm and challenge, you review
Multi-platform support highlighted (Codex CLI, Aider, Gemini CLI)
Game UI shown as visual proof, not headline feature
Custom AI agents with personality system explained
Image placeholders for CEO to fill later
failure_looks_like: Pipeline explanation is superficial without research connections
Screenshots are broken links or missing placeholders
Comparison includes coding assistants (Cursor, Cline) instead of agent frameworks
dod.quality_bar: Marketing-quality README that makes developers stop scrolling, backed by research references that establish credibility. Factual and specific, not hype-driven.

output.summary: Created directive files with DOD extraction.
All context loaded: vision, lessons, design docs, agent registry.
10 differentiators, 10 messaging gaps, 3 existing assets. Critical gap: no dashboard panel screenshots. Multi-platform adapters undocumented.

brainstorm.agent: Sarah Chen, Marcus Rivera, Priya Sharma

output.summary: Full strategic brainstorm with deliberation. 8 convergence points, 3 resolved disagreements, 3 CEO clarifications answered.

CEO verified intent: no 'revolutionary' language, image placeholders (CEO captures later), competitor comparison against agent frameworks only (research Manus etc.), pipeline diagram with worked example.

verified_intent.goal: Rewrite README.md as a marketing-quality technical document with Mermaid pipeline diagram (including worked example), inline research citations, agent framework competitor comparison, and image placeholders for CEO to fill later.
constraints: Image placeholders only — CEO will capture screenshots/GIF personally
Competitor comparison: agent frameworks only (CrewAI, AutoGen, Devin, Manus, others to research). No coding assistants (Cursor, Cline, aider)
Pipeline diagram must include a worked example showing a real directive flowing through steps
Research citations must be inline links with plain-language context, not footnotes
Target 250-350 visible lines with collapsible sections for depth
Mermaid for pipeline diagram (GitHub renders natively)
Game UI as visual hook, not headline feature
verified_intent.quality_bar: Marketing-quality README that makes developers stop scrolling, backed by research references that establish credibility. Factual and specific, not hype-driven.

acceptance_scenarios.given: A developer lands on the GitHub repo from a Twitter/HN link
acceptance_scenarios.then: They understand gruai is an autonomous AI company framework with research-grounded pipeline

acceptance_scenarios.given: A developer scrolls to the pipeline section
acceptance_scenarios.when: They see the Mermaid diagram with worked example
acceptance_scenarios.then: They understand the 15-step flow and how weight adaptation works, with research citations inline
They see how gruai differs from CrewAI, AutoGen, Devin, Manus on specific dimensions

acceptance_scenarios.scenario: Image placeholders are ready for CEO
acceptance_scenarios.then: They see clear placeholder comments indicating where to add hero GIF, dashboard screenshot, game screenshot, pipeline screenshot

out_of_scope: Capturing actual screenshots or GIF — CEO will do this
Creating a separate landing page or documentation site
Changing any source code — README.md only
Comparing against coding assistants (Cursor, Cline, aider, SWE-Agent)

modifications: CEO modified: screenshots/GIF — use placeholders, CEO captures later
CEO modified: competitor scope — agent frameworks only, research Manus and others
CEO added: pipeline diagram should include a worked example

output.summary: Single project plan: Taylor (Content Builder) builds, Priya (CMO) reviews. Moderate complexity. Cast validated.
CEO approved with one modification: multi-platform adapters mentioned as roadmap/in-development, NOT as shipped feature.
modifications: Multi-platform support changed from feature claim to roadmap mention

output.summary: Decomposed into 2 sequential tasks: (1) research competitors + verify citation URLs, (2) write complete README. Task 2 depends on task 1. Project.json validated.
Created branch directive/readme-rewrite. No worktree needed (uncommitted changes don't overlap with README.md).

Both tasks completed. Task 1: researched 7 competitors, verified 7 citation URLs. Task 2: wrote complete README.md (263 visible lines, 364 total). Priya (CMO) reviewed: PASS on all 10 DOD criteria, 0 critical issues, 9 non-blocking suggestions.
validate-reviews.sh passed. Review artifact present for write-readme task. No self-review, no self-certification. All DOD criteria verified by reviewer (Priya).
Digest generated. No follow-ups needed (content-only directive). Stale doc detection ran — no actionable stale docs. No design doc or lesson updates warranted.
